title: Vice President - Intelligent Automation And Data Platforms

mustHave.text: 10+ years in engineering, DevOps, platform engineering, SRE, or similar technical leadership roles
mustHave.interviewerLens: Probe the scale and scope of teams/platforms led — number of engineers managed, global vs. regional scope, and progression from IC to VP-level leadership.

mustHave.text: Strong knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), infrastructure-as-code, CI/CD, and modern DevSecOps
mustHave.interviewerLens: Expect scenario-based questions on architectural decisions, toolchain choices (e.g., Terraform, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD), and how they've embedded security into delivery pipelines.

mustHave.text: Experience converting strategy and vision into products and platforms with high adoption rates
mustHave.interviewerLens: Probe for product thinking in a platform context — how they measured adoption, handled low-adoption failures, and iterated based on developer feedback.

mustHave.text: Ability to lead through ambiguity and create clarity in complex global environments
mustHave.interviewerLens: Ask for examples where requirements were ill-defined or org structure was unclear — how they built alignment and drove toward outcomes without full authority.

mustHave.text: Drive cross-functional alignment with Security, Cloud, and Engineering
mustHave.interviewerLens: Look for evidence of influencing without direct authority across org boundaries, particularly where security and engineering priorities conflicted.

mustHave.text: Architect and scale an Automated Service Catalog for standardized services across regions
mustHave.interviewerLens: Probe depth of experience with internal developer platforms (Backstage, ServiceNow, or custom), multi-region rollout challenges, and governance models.

niceToHave.text: Advanced degree in Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
niceToHave.interviewerLens: Unlikely to be a strong filter given the depth of experience required; may surface in exec-level conversations about thought leadership.
Probe familiarity with regulated environments, compliance constraints on automation pipelines, and audit requirements — implicitly valued even if not stated.

niceToHave.text: Establishing a federated contribution model for global developer communities
niceToHave.interviewerLens: Look for inner-source or open-source governance experience — how they structured contribution standards, review processes, and quality gates at scale.

culture.text: High-growth environment demanding both strategy and execution ownership
Cross-functional partnership across Security, Cloud, and Engineering
Continuous feedback loops with engineering teams
Strong DEI focus and people-first approach
Work/life balance and employee wellness emphasized
Global developer community with federated autonomy model

implicitBar: Despite the VP title, the role demands a hands-on technical architect who has personally built and scaled internal developer platforms, not merely overseen them — candidates without direct platform product ownership at global scale will likely be screened out.

likelyStages.focus: Years of experience, leadership scope, compensation alignment, visa/location eligibility, and high-level platform engineering background.

likelyStages.format: 60-min video call with hiring manager or senior leader
likelyStages.focus: Technical depth in DevSecOps and platform engineering, strategic vision for automation, and examples of cross-functional influence.

likelyStages.format: Panel — multiple 45-60 min interviews with engineering, security, and product stakeholders
likelyStages.focus: System design for internal developer platform, behavioral leadership scenarios, cultural fit, and cross-functional collaboration evidence.

likelyStages.format: 30-45 min with C-suite or divisional CTO/CIO
likelyStages.focus: Strategic alignment, ability to translate technical roadmap to business outcomes, and executive presence / communication style.

redFlagsForCandidate: 'High-growth environment' combined with VP-level scope may signal that foundational platform infrastructure is immature and the candidate will need to build from scratch with limited resources.
Role posted through a third-party recruiter ('our client') with company name withheld — limits ability to research actual culture, stability, or leadership tenure.
'No visa sponsorship available' and 'US-based only' flag a narrow talent pool, potentially indicating urgency or prior failed searches.
'Drive cross-functional alignment' without mention of direct engineering headcount may indicate this is an influential-but-not-line-management role with accountability but limited authority.
Compensation ceiling of $200K for a VP in Finance & Insurance with global scope may be below market for the seniority implicitly required.

questionsToExpect.prompt: Design an Automated Service Catalog that global developers across multiple regions can contribute to and consume from. Walk me through your architecture, governance model, and how you'd ensure quality.
questionsToExpect.why: Directly maps to the core deliverable of the role; tests architectural depth and platform product thinking simultaneously.

questionsToExpect.prompt: Tell me about a time you drove adoption of an internal platform that initially had low uptake. What did you learn and how did you turn it around?
questionsToExpect.why: The JD specifically calls out 'high adoption rates' — interviewers will probe for product iteration skills and developer empathy.

questionsToExpect.prompt: Describe a situation where you had to align Security, Engineering, and Cloud teams on an architecture decision where they had conflicting priorities. How did you reach consensus?
questionsToExpect.why: Cross-functional alignment with exactly these three groups is called out explicitly — expect a deep dive here.

questionsToExpect.prompt: You've just joined and discovered the automation strategy is fragmented — five teams are using different IaC tools with no standardization. How do you approach the first 90 days?
questionsToExpect.why: Tests the candidate's ability to 'lead through ambiguity and create clarity' as stated in qualifications, and reveals strategic planning skills.

questionsToExpect.prompt: How would you embed security gates into a CI/CD pipeline for a regulated financial services environment without significantly slowing deployment velocity?
questionsToExpect.why: Finance & Insurance context makes DevSecOps compliance a non-negotiable; tests ability to balance speed and governance.

questionsToExpect.prompt: Tell me about the largest platform modernization initiative you've led. What was the scope, how did you measure success, and what would you do differently?
questionsToExpect.why: The word 'modernization' leads the responsibilities list — interviewers want evidence of end-to-end ownership at scale.

questionsToExpect.prompt: How would you establish a federated contribution model that allows developers across 10+ countries to publish automations while maintaining standards and preventing regressions?
questionsToExpect.why: Directly tests experience with inner-source governance, a nuanced and differentiating requirement in the JD.

questionsToExpect.prompt: Give me an example of how you built a continuous feedback loop with engineering teams to track and act on measurable outcomes. What metrics did you use and how did they influence your roadmap?
questionsToExpect.why: Measurable outcomes are called out twice in the JD — interviewers will want concrete metrics and iteration evidence, not vague process descriptions.

storyPrompts: A time you built or scaled an internal developer platform from low adoption to widespread use — what drove the turnaround.
A situation where you had to create alignment between Security and Engineering teams who had fundamentally different risk tolerances on an automation or DevSecOps initiative.
A complex platform modernization you led under ambiguity — how you defined the roadmap when requirements were unclear and stakeholders were misaligned.
A time you established a federated or inner-source contribution model across a distributed, global engineering organization.
A moment when you had to influence a strategic direction at the VP or C-suite level without having direct authority over all the teams involved.
A time you designed or redesigned a CI/CD or infrastructure-as-code architecture for a regulated industry — what constraints you navigated and what tradeoffs you made.
A situation where a platform you owned failed to deliver measurable business outcomes — how you diagnosed it, communicated upward, and course-corrected.
A time you had to balance long-term platform vision with short-term engineering team demands, and how you negotiated priorities with stakeholders.
